notes: Structural drying on education/institutional campuses (lecture halls, libraries, laboratories). Show real drying equipment deployed by a professional: air movers, refrigerant dehumidifiers, moisture meters, compartmentalised containment zones. Bright, clean, Australian, metric. No on-image text, no logos.

images.context: Course hero — drying equipment deployed in an Australian campus auditorium/lecture hall
images.prompt: A professional water-damage restoration technician in hi-vis and gloves deploying a neat row of air movers and a large refrigerant dehumidifier across the floor of a bright, empty Australian university lecture hall with tiered seating and a hardwood stage, drying equipment carefully positioned, safety barrier tape across the entrance. Clean, organised, professional, natural daylight. Realistic photography, wide mid-distance framing.
images.negativePrompt: no on-image text, no logos, no brand names, no watermarks, no gore, no distressing imagery, no patients, no identifiable face-forward portraits, no students present, no US power outlets, no imperial-only units

images.context: Module 1 & 5 — moisture-meter readings in a campus library or laboratory
images.prompt: Over-the-shoulder close-up of a technician's gloved hand pressing a handheld moisture meter against a wall in a bright Australian campus library with book stacks, reading moisture content, an air mover running low in the frame. Hands-on-task framing, methodical and precise. Realistic photography.

images.context: Module 5 — compartmentalised incremental drying zone in an institutional corridor
images.prompt: A compartmentalised drying containment zone set up in a bright Australian institutional corridor, sectioned off with clear polyethylene sheeting and safety signage on stands, a refrigerant dehumidifier and stacked air movers running inside the isolated zone while the adjacent corridor stays open and clean. Orderly, staged, professional, natural light. Realistic photography, mid-distance framing.
